title: Pan Seared Crunchy Jamaican Almond Skillet
description: A nostalgic Jamaican dish featuring flaked almonds and a yuzu cure, made in an iron teppan skillet with an umami and crunchy texture, following ayurvedic sattvic friendly principles.
instructions: Heat the iron teppan skillet over medium heat and add the coconut oil.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and saute for 1 minute.
Add the toasted flaked almonds and cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the thinly sliced scallions and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
In a small bowl, whisk together the yuzu juice, turmeric, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the yuzu mixture over the almond mixture and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring frequently, until the mixture is crunchy and fragrant.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool for 1 minute.
Serve the pan seared crunchy Jamaican almond skillet hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
